meaning: vt.灞曞紑,灞曠ず,鍏紑,浣挎棗鎷涘睍vi.鎵撳紑,灞曞紑,鏄鹃湶
examples: Once outside the inner breakwater, we began to unfurl all the sails.
The dramatic changes in Europe continue to unfurl.
Imagine a yoga mat you unfurl from a metal tube that's actually a stereo.
Meals unfurl with great ceremony - they are joyful, boisterous and almost inevitably communal.
Race days unfurl with an introduction to the day's programme and to the competing teams.
